question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 18?

fragility_points: A macro or crypto-specific selloff before April 18 could quickly erase the cushion.
Any ambiguity in the exact noon-ET candle mapping would reduce confidence.
This estimate relies partly on daily-close history as a proxy for a minute-specific contract.
key_assumptions: BTC stays in roughly the current trading regime through April 18 noon ET.
No major macro, regulatory, or liquidation shock pushes BTC back below 70k at the resolving minute.
Polymarket wording and Binance candle mechanics map cleanly to the intended noon-ET observation.
main_logical_chain: The contract is a narrow timestamped Binance BTC/USDT close, so exact resolution mechanics matter.
Current Binance spot around 74.2k makes Yes favored because BTC is already comfortably above 70k.
Recent Binance history shows >70k is common but not persistent enough to justify near-certainty.
Therefore Yes is more likely than No, but a disciplined outside-view estimate should sit well below the market's ~90%.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is more likely than No because BTC/USDT is currently well above 70k, but the market's roughly 90% pricing overstates confidence for a strict single-minute Binance noon-ET close four days away.
quote_anchors: Yes is more likely than No, but the market looks too confident.
For Yes to resolve... the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle labeled 12:00 in ET on April 18 must close above 70,000.
payload.source_quality_view: Source quality is solid for this case: Polymarket is authoritative for contract wording and Binance is authoritative for exchange mechanics and price data; independence is reasonably high and source-of-truth ambiguity is medium-low.
strongest_disconfirmers: In the most recent 30 daily closes through April 14, BTC closed above 70k only 15 of 30 times.
The contract resolves on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, not on daily close or intraday highs.
BTC can move several thousand dollars over a few days, leaving meaningful downside path risk.
strongest_supports: Binance live BTCUSDT spot was about 74,159, leaving roughly a $4.2k cushion above the threshold.
There are only four days until resolution, so continuation above 70k is favored absent a new shock.
Recent Binance history shows BTC has often traded above 70k, so the threshold is in a live but favorable regime.
payload.timing_relevance: Resolution is specifically the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle close at 12:00 ET on 2026-04-18, making date, timezone, and timestamp interpretation materially important.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact chart/UI labeling of the noon-ET minute on Binance was not directly observed, though docs support the kline mechanics.
Daily-close frequency is only an approximation for the resolving 1-minute close.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move up if BTC holds a robust 74k-75k+ cushion into late April 17 or April 18, and move down sharply if BTC falls back toward 71k or a fresh shock increases odds of a sub-70k noon print.
